text:
KULICKE & SOFFA INDUSTRIES, INC. ( $KLIC)

Kapsamlı Şirket Değerleme ve Stratejik Analiz 

1. Yönetici Özeti ve Stratejik Yol Ayrımı

Yarı iletken endüstrisinde Moore Kanunu'nun fiziksel sınırlara dayanması ve ön uç (front-end) litografi maliyetlerinin katlanarak artması, performans artışının yeni odağını İleri Seviye Paketleme  ve Heterojen Entegrrasyon süreçlerine kaydırmıştır. Kulicke & Soffa, tarihsel olarak otomotiv, endüstriyel ve tüketici elektroniğine hitap eden olgun tel bağlama pazarında %80’e yakın pazar payıyla ezici bir cash cow yönetmektedir. Ancak şirket, son iki yıldır akademik ve kurumsal analizlere konu olan (örneğin 2025 sonundaki Harvard Business School stratejik vaka analizindeki gibi) çok kritik bir yol ayrımındadır: Olgun pazarlardaki nakit akışını koruyarak, yapay zeka (AI) ve yüksek performanslı bilgi işlem (HPC) çiplerinin mimarisini belirleyen ileri seviye montaj teknolojilerine liderlik etmek.

FY2026 ikinci çeyrek sonuçları ve güncel pazar dinamikleri, KLIC’in bu teknolojik dönüşümü başarıyla gerçekleştirdiğini ve döngüsel bir dipten çıkış (cyclical inflection) dönemine girdiğini doğrulamaktadır. Şirket, karlılığı düşük olan Electronics Assembly iş kolunu tamamen kapatarak odağını netleştirmiş, yapay zeka veri merkezlerindeki bant genişliği kısıtlamalarını çözen Thermo-Compression Bonding ve Silicon Photonics çözümlerinde vites artırmıştır.

2. Finansal Performans ve Döngüsel Kaldıraç Analizi

KLIC’in finansal yapısı, arka uç (back-end) sermaye ekipmanı üreticilerinde görülen yüksek operasyonel kaldıraç karakteristiğini taşımaktadır. Sektörel envanter düzeltmelerinin sona ermesiyle birlikte, gelir artışı marjlar üzerinde çarpan etkisi yaratmaktadır.

Çeyreklik Finansal Gerçekleşmeler ve Dinamikler

-- Gelir İvmesi: Q2 FY2026 net geliri, bir önceki çeyreğe göre %21.5, geçen yılın aynı dönemine göre ise %49.8 artarak 242.6 milyon USD seviyesine ulaşmıştır. Bu rakam, konsensüs beklentisi olan 191.4 milyon USD’yi büyük bir sürprizle geride bırakmıştır.

-- Brüt Kar Marjı Genişlemesi: Brüt marj %49.3 olarak gerçekleşmiştir. Geçen yılın aynı çeyreğindeki %24.9'luk seviyeye kıyasla 2,440 baz puanlık operasyonel genişleme, yüksek marjlı Advanced Solutions ve bellek ürün miksinin devreye girmesiyle açıklanmaktadır.

-- Karlılığa Dönüş: GAAP operasyonel karı 38.5 milyon USD'ye yükselmiştir. Geçen yılın aynı döneminde kaydedilen ağır zararların ardından, Non-GAAP operasyonel kar marjı %19.1’e (46.3 milyon USD) fırlamıştır. Non-GAAP EPS ise 0.79 USD ile beklentilerin 0.12 USD üzerinde gelmiştir.

Q3 ve Q4 FY2026 Öngörüleri

Şirketin resmi Q3 FY2026 kılavuzu, ardışık olarak %28 büyüme ile 310 milyon USD gelir hedeflemektedir. Operasyonel giderlerin (OpEx) 85 milyon USD seviyesine çıkması, üretim hacmini (production ramp) desteklemek için yapılan mühendislik istihdamları ve değişken performans primlerinden kaynaklanmaktadır. Yönetim, Q4 FY2026 döneminde de Q3'e kıyasla %5 ile %10 arasında organik bir büyüme trendinin korunacağını öngörmektedir. Bu doğrultuda FY2026 toplam geliri yaklaşık 1.07 milyar USD bandına doğru ilerlemektedir.

3. Segment Bazında Operasyonel ve Teknolojik Derinleşme

KLIC’in ürün portföyü, yarı iletken dünyasının hem olgun hem de en uç teknolojilerine eş zamanlı hizmet verecek şekilde yeniden yapılandırılmıştır.

-- Olgun Pazarlar: Ball/Wedge Bonding ──> Nakit Üretimi & Hacim (Akıllı Telefon, Otomotiv, Endüstriyel)

-- Seküler Pazarlar: Advanced Solutions ──> TCB, FTC, ProMEM Suite, Vertical Wire (AI, HPC, HBM)

-- İstikrar Segmenti: APS ──> Fabrika Kullanım Oranlarına Bağlı Yedek Parça ve Servis Güvencesi

A. Geleneksel ve Hafif Döngüsel Segmentler

-- Ball Bonding Equipment (Tarihsel Pay: %44.8): Klasik altın ve bakır tel bağlama sistemleridir. Akıllı telefon, PC ve alt segment tüketici elektroniğindeki döngüsel toparlanma, bu segmenti Q2 FY2026'da yeniden ana gelir sürücülerinden biri haline getirmiştir (GenSemi gelirleri çeyreklik %19.4 artışla 148.9 milyon USD olmuştur).

-- Wedge Bonding Equipment (Tarihsel Pay: %16.9): Güç yarı iletkenleri, elektrikli araç (EV) batarya paketleri ve endüstriyel modüllere hizmet eder. Otomotiv sektöründeki elektrifikasyon trendiyle daha stabil bir grafik çizmektedir.

-- Aftermarket Products & Services (APS) (Tarihsel Pay: %23.9): Küresel kurulu makine parkının sarf malzemeleri ve servis hizmetleridir. Donanım satışları düşse dahi, fabrikaların çalışmasına paralel olarak sürekli nakit üreten, döngüselliği en düşük defansif kalkandır.

B. Yeni Nesil Hafıza (Memory) ve Güç Çözümleri (2026 İnovasyonları)

Bellek segmenti, kurumsal SSD (eSSD) ve yüksek performanslı sunucu talebiyle Q2 FY2026'da çeyreklik bazda %93 büyüme kaydederek 31.3 milyon USD'ye ulaşmıştır. Şirket bu ivmeyi kalıcı kılmak adına Mart 2026'da iki büyük teknolojik silahını duyurmuştur:

1. ProMEM Suite: Standart yüksek hacimli bellek montajında (Stacked NAND) üretkenliği ve proses kontrolünü dramatik şekilde artıran bir ball bonding yazılım ve donanım paketidir.

2. Vertical Wire Teknolojisi (ATP MEM PLUS): Geleneksel tel bağlamayı dikey boyuta taşıyarak ara bağlantı yoğunluğunu artırır ve paket ayak izini küçültür. Stacked DRAM ve yeni nesil yüksek yoğunluklu bellek mimarilerinde silikon delikli geçişlere (TSV) karşı son derece maliyet etkin bir alternatif sunmaktadır.

4. Gelişmiş Paketleme ve AI Altyapı Katalizörü

Advanced Solutions segmenti altındaki Thermo-Compression Bonding (TCB), KLIC'in uzun vadeli seküler büyüme motorudur. Şirket, TCB iş kolunun FY2026’da çeyreklik bazda en az %70 büyüyeceğini ve tek başına 100 milyon USD+ barajını aşacağını doğrulamıştır.

Fluxless TCB (FTC) ve Teknik Üstünlük

Gelişmiş veri merkezi işlemcilerinde kullanılan alt bileşenlerin kalınlıkları azaldıkça ve kalıp boyutları büyüdükçe (30 mm ve üzeri), geleneksel "flip-chip" yöntemleri termal eğrilme ve hizalama hatalarına neden olmaktadır. KLIC’in Fluxless TCB (FTC) teknolojisi bu problemi şu inovasyonlarla çözmektedir:

-- Mikron Altı Hassasiyet: Lehim tabanlı bağlantıları 10 μm hatveye (pitch), doğrudan bakır-bakır bağlantıları ise en az 5 μm hatveye kadar indirebilmektedir.

-- Formik Asit Buharı Oksit İndirgeme: Süreç esnasında sıvı akı (flux) kullanılmadığı için kalıplar arasında neredeyse "sıfır boşluk yüksekliği" elde edilir; bu da elektriksel direnci düşürür ve termal yönetimi optimize eder.

-- Co-Packaged Optics ve Silikon Fotonik: Yapay zeka kümelerindeki anahtarlama ve ağ hızlarını artırmak için optik transceiver bileşenlerinin doğrudan işlemci yanına TCB ile montajlanması sürecinde KLIC, küresel lider dökümhanelerden ve OSAT'lardan peş peşe siparişler almaktadır.

5. Kapasite Genişletme ve Gelecek Projeksiyonları

Advanced Solutions segmentinin ölçeklenebilmesi için üretim darboğazlarının aşılması gerekmektedir. Şirket yönetimi bu doğrultuda stratejik bir sermaye hamlesi yapmıştır:

1. CapEx Artırımı
CapEx Artırımı:FY2026 Dönemi.Şirket, yıllık sermaye harcaması (CapEx) bütçesini 12 milyon USD seviyesinden 22 milyon USD seviyesine neredeyse iki katına çıkarmıştır.

2. Üretim Hatlarının Modernizasyonu:
Q3/Q4 FY2026. Artırılan bütçe doğrudan Singapur ve ilgili üretim tesislerinde Fluxless TCB ve dikey tel montaj hatlarının otomasyon kalitesini yükseltmek için kullanılmaktadır.

3.Kapasite Hedefine Ulaşılması:
Erken FY2027. Yapılan bu yatırımlarla Advanced Solutions segmentinin yıllık üretim ve sevkiyat kapasitesi run-rate bazında 400 milyon USD satış potansiyeline ulaştırılacaktır.

Sipariş birikimini gösteren backlog yapısı da bu süreci desteklemektedir ve hızla artmakdadır. Siparişlerin iptal edilebilir olması bir risk olsa da Çin, Tayvan ve Kore'deki fabrika kullanım oranlarının %90'ın üzerinde seyretmesi, bu siparişlerin büyük ölçüde realizasyonla sonuçlanacağını göstermektedir.

6. Sektörel Karşılaştırma ve İleri Dönük Değerleme Modeli

Yarı iletken ekipman pazarında değerleme yapılırken ön uç (ASML, KLA, Lam Research, Applied Materials) ve arka uç (KLIC, Besi, ASMPT) ayrımı kritik rol oynar. Ön uç firmaları daha az döngüsel ve yüksek bariyerli oldukları için tarihsel olarak yüksek çarpanlara sahiptir.

KLIC ise tarihsel olarak olgun wire bonder pazarının dalgalanmalarına maruz kaldığı için düşük çarpanla işlem görmüştür. Fakat Advanced Solutions segmentinin kapasitesinin 400 milyon USD'ye ayarlanması ve TCB'nin yüksek karlılığı, çarpan genişlemesini tetiklemektedir.

Akran Grubu Karşılaştırma Analizi (FOTO - 1)

2027 Gelir ve Çarpan Projeksiyonu

-- FY2026 Tahmini Gelir: ~1.07 milyar USD (Mevcut çeyreklik gidişat ve Q3 kılavuzu tabanlı).

-- FY2027 Projeksiyon Geliri: Bellek toparlanması, ProMEM Suite adaptasyonu ve TCB'nin yıllık 100 milyon USD'lik barajı kırıp Advanced Solutions run-rate'ini yukarı taşımasıyla 1.25 milyar USD (%17-20 civarı bir yıllık büyüme).

-- Değerleme Yaklaşımı: Advanced Packaging payının artması sebebiyle şirkete hak ettiği tarihsel iskontonun üzerinde, 6x Forward P/S (İleri Dönük Satış) çarpanı uygulanması rasyoneldir. Bu çarpan altında şirketin adil kurumsal değeri 7.5 milyar USD olarak hesaplanmaktadır.

Şirketin kasasındaki 254.3 milyon USD net nakit ve sıfır borç yapısı, hisse başına değer hesaplamasında ekstra bir emniyet marjı sunmaktadır. Finansal modelleme kapsamında %8 iskonto oranı tatbik edildiğinde, hisse senedinin önümüzdeki 12 aylık periyotta 135 USD adil değer hedefine doğru re-rate edilmesi beklenmektedir. Bu da mevcut konsolide fiyata göre yaklaşık %20 yukarı potansiyel anlamına gelir.

7. Derinlemesine Risk ve Tehdit Analizi

Risk Matrisi ve Stratejik Tehditler: KLIC pozisyonlanmasında aşağı yönlü risklerin yakından izlenmesi gerekmektedir.

I. İkame Teknolojiler ve Hibrit Bağlama Tehdidi

En uç seviye AI hızlandırıcılarında (NVIDIA, AMD vb. yeni nesil mimarileri) TCB hatvelerinin dahi yetersiz kaldığı noktalarda Hybrid Bonding (Doğrudan Bakır-Bakır Die-to-Wafer entegrasyonu) devreye girmektedir. Bu alanda Hollandalı BE Semiconductor (Besi) tartışmasız pazar lideridir ve ana dökümhanelerin (TSMC vb.) onaylı tedarikçisidir.

Risk: Eğer Hybrid Bonding teknolojisi beklenenden daha hızlı ucuzlar ve orta segment çiplere de hızla adapte edilirse, KLIC'in TCB pazarı daralabilir. KLIC, 2024'ten bu yana kendi bünyesindeki Hibrit Bağlama AR-GE programını hızlandırdığını ve erken müşteri test aşamasında olduğunu (Mart 2026 güncellemelerinde) belirtse de Besi ile olan teknolojik ve zamansal makası kapatamama riski mevcuttur.

II. Müşteri Konsantrasyonu ve Kamçı Etkisi
KLIC’in gelir yapısı son derece dikeydir. En büyük 10 müşteri (büyük OSAT'lar olan ASE, Amkor ve dev IDM'ler) toplam grup gelirlerinin %55'ini oluşturmaktadır.

Risk: Son tüketici elektroniğindeki ani bir makroekonomik durgunluk, bu 10 dev firmanın CapEx bütçelerini anında dondurmasına yol açar. Arka uç üreticileri, tedarik zincirinin en sonunda yer aldığından "Kamçı Etkisi" nedeniyle sipariş iptallerini veya ertelemelerini en şiddetli hisseden grup olmaktadır.

III. Jeopolitik Sıkışma ve Doğu Asya Riski

KLIC her ne kadar Singapur merkezli ve ABD operasyonlu bir yapıya sahip olsa da en büyük yarı iletken montaj müşterileri ve dökümhaneleri Tayvan, Çin Halk Cumhuriyeti ve Güney Kore sınırları içerisindedir.

Risk: ABD-Çin ticaret savaşlarının gelişmiş paketleme ekipmanlarına (özellikle TCB ve alt bileşenlerine) yönelik getirebileceği ek ihracat yasakları veya Tayvan Boğazı'ndaki olası bir lojistik aksama, şirketin backlog teslimatlarını tamamen felç edebilir.

IV. CapEx ve Marj Baskısı

Yıllık CapEx'in 12 milyon USD'den 22 milyon USD'ye çıkarılması, kısa vadede nakit akışını ve serbest nakit akışı marjlarını (Q2 FY2026'da düzeltilmiş serbest nakit akışı 6.3 milyon USD seviyesindedir) baskılamaktadır. Yeni hatların verimlilik oranlarında yaşanabilecek herhangi bir operasyonel aksaklık, Q3 ve Q4 brüt marj tahminlerini aşağı yönlü revize ettirebilir.

8. Stratejik Sonuç

Kulicke & Soffa, döngüsel bir çöküşün ardından operasyonel karlılığa dönüş hikayesini başarıyla yazmıştır. Şirket, geleneksel iş kollarının sağladığı güçlü nakit akışını, geleceğin AI ve bellek standartları olan TCB, ProMEM ve dikey tel teknolojilerine başarıyla aktarmaktadır. Hisse senedinde son aylarda yaşanan güçlü momentum ve ralli, bu döngüsel dönüşün pazar tarafından satın alınmaya başladığını gösterse de FY2027 projeksiyonlarımız doğrultusunda şirketin çarpan büyümesinin henüz tamamlanmadığı değerlendirilmektedir. Bilançosunda borç bulunmayan, düzenli çeyreklik temettü ödeyen ve hisse geri alımlarıyla paydaş değerini destekleyen KLIC, uzun vadeli yarı iletken portföylerinde temel bir arka uç pozisyonu olarak öne çıkmaktadır.

Yatırım tavsiyesi değildir.

Kapsamlı Şirket Değerleme ve Stratejik Analiz 

1. Yönetici Özeti ve Stratejik Yol Ayrımı

Yarı iletken endüstrisinde Moore Kanunu'nun fiziksel sınırlara dayanması ve ön uç (front-end) litografi maliyetlerinin katlanarak artması, performans artışının yeni odağını İleri Seviye Paketleme  ve Heterojen Entegrrasyon süreçlerine kaydırmıştır. Kulicke & Soffa, tarihsel olarak otomotiv, endüstriyel ve tüketici elektroniğine hitap eden olgun tel bağlama pazarında %80’e yakın pazar payıyla ezici bir cash cow yönetmektedir. Ancak şirket, son iki yıldır akademik ve kurumsal analizlere konu olan (örneğin 2025 sonundaki Harvard Business School stratejik vaka analizindeki gibi) çok kritik bir yol ayrımındadır: Olgun pazarlardaki nakit akışını koruyarak, yapay zeka (AI) ve yüksek performanslı bilgi işlem (HPC) çiplerinin mimarisini belirleyen ileri seviye montaj teknolojilerine liderlik etmek.

FY2026 ikinci çeyrek sonuçları ve güncel pazar dinamikleri, KLIC’in bu teknolojik dönüşümü başarıyla gerçekleştirdiğini ve döngüsel bir dipten çıkış (cyclical inflection) dönemine girdiğini doğrulamaktadır. Şirket, karlılığı düşük olan Electronics Assembly iş kolunu tamamen kapatarak odağını netleştirmiş, yapay zeka veri merkezlerindeki bant genişliği kısıtlamalarını çözen Thermo-Compression Bonding ve Silicon Photonics çözümlerinde vites artırmıştır.

2. Finansal Performans ve Döngüsel Kaldıraç Analizi

KLIC’in finansal yapısı, arka uç (back-end) sermaye ekipmanı üreticilerinde görülen yüksek operasyonel kaldıraç karakteristiğini taşımaktadır. Sektörel envanter düzeltmelerinin sona ermesiyle birlikte, gelir artışı marjlar üzerinde çarpan etkisi yaratmaktadır.

Çeyreklik Finansal Gerçekleşmeler ve Dinamikler

-- Gelir İvmesi: Q2 FY2026 net geliri, bir önceki çeyreğe göre %21.5, geçen yılın aynı dönemine göre ise %49.8 artarak 242.6 milyon USD seviyesine ulaşmıştır. Bu rakam, konsensüs beklentisi olan 191.4 milyon USD’yi büyük bir sürprizle geride bırakmıştır.

-- Brüt Kar Marjı Genişlemesi: Brüt marj %49.3 olarak gerçekleşmiştir. Geçen yılın aynı çeyreğindeki %24.9'luk seviyeye kıyasla 2,440 baz puanlık operasyonel genişleme, yüksek marjlı Advanced Solutions ve bellek ürün miksinin devreye girmesiyle açıklanmaktadır.

-- Karlılığa Dönüş: GAAP operasyonel karı 38.5 milyon USD'ye yükselmiştir. Geçen yılın aynı döneminde kaydedilen ağır zararların ardından, Non-GAAP operasyonel kar marjı %19.1’e (46.3 milyon USD) fırlamıştır. Non-GAAP EPS ise 0.79 USD ile beklentilerin 0.12 USD üzerinde gelmiştir.

Q3 ve Q4 FY2026 Öngörüleri

Şirketin resmi Q3 FY2026 kılavuzu, ardışık olarak %28 büyüme ile 310 milyon USD gelir hedeflemektedir. Operasyonel giderlerin (OpEx) 85 milyon USD seviyesine çıkması, üretim hacmini (production ramp) desteklemek için yapılan mühendislik istihdamları ve değişken performans primlerinden kaynaklanmaktadır. Yönetim, Q4 FY2026 döneminde de Q3'e kıyasla %5 ile %10 arasında organik bir büyüme trendinin korunacağını öngörmektedir. Bu doğrultuda FY2026 toplam geliri yaklaşık 1.07 milyar USD bandına doğru ilerlemektedir.

3. Segment Bazında Operasyonel ve Teknolojik Derinleşme

KLIC’in ürün portföyü, yarı iletken dünyasının hem olgun hem de en uç teknolojilerine eş zamanlı hizmet verecek şekilde yeniden yapılandırılmıştır.

-- Olgun Pazarlar: Ball/Wedge Bonding ──> Nakit Üretimi & Hacim (Akıllı Telefon, Otomotiv, Endüstriyel)

-- Seküler Pazarlar: Advanced Solutions ──> TCB, FTC, ProMEM Suite, Vertical Wire (AI, HPC, HBM)

-- İstikrar Segmenti: APS ──> Fabrika Kullanım Oranlarına Bağlı Yedek Parça ve Servis Güvencesi

A. Geleneksel ve Hafif Döngüsel Segmentler

-- Ball Bonding Equipment (Tarihsel Pay: %44.8): Klasik altın ve bakır tel bağlama sistemleridir. Akıllı telefon, PC ve alt segment tüketici elektroniğindeki döngüsel toparlanma, bu segmenti Q2 FY2026'da yeniden ana gelir sürücülerinden biri haline getirmiştir (GenSemi gelirleri çeyreklik %19.4 artışla 148.9 milyon USD olmuştur).

-- Wedge Bonding Equipment (Tarihsel Pay: %16.9): Güç yarı iletkenleri, elektrikli araç (EV) batarya paketleri ve endüstriyel modüllere hizmet eder. Otomotiv sektöründeki elektrifikasyon trendiyle daha stabil bir grafik çizmektedir.

-- Aftermarket Products & Services (APS) (Tarihsel Pay: %23.9): Küresel kurulu makine parkının sarf malzemeleri ve servis hizmetleridir. Donanım satışları düşse dahi, fabrikaların çalışmasına paralel olarak sürekli nakit üreten, döngüselliği en düşük defansif kalkandır.

B. Yeni Nesil Hafıza (Memory) ve Güç Çözümleri (2026 İnovasyonları)

Bellek segmenti, kurumsal SSD (eSSD) ve yüksek performanslı sunucu talebiyle Q2 FY2026'da çeyreklik bazda %93 büyüme kaydederek 31.3 milyon USD'ye ulaşmıştır. Şirket bu ivmeyi kalıcı kılmak adına Mart 2026'da iki büyük teknolojik silahını duyurmuştur:

1. ProMEM Suite: Standart yüksek hacimli bellek montajında (Stacked NAND) üretkenliği ve proses kontrolünü dramatik şekilde artıran bir ball bonding yazılım ve donanım paketidir.

2. Vertical Wire Teknolojisi (ATP MEM PLUS): Geleneksel tel bağlamayı dikey boyuta taşıyarak ara bağlantı yoğunluğunu artırır ve paket ayak izini küçültür. Stacked DRAM ve yeni nesil yüksek yoğunluklu bellek mimarilerinde silikon delikli geçişlere (TSV) karşı son derece maliyet etkin bir alternatif sunmaktadır.

4. Gelişmiş Paketleme ve AI Altyapı Katalizörü

Advanced Solutions segmenti altındaki Thermo-Compression Bonding (TCB), KLIC'in uzun vadeli seküler büyüme motorudur. Şirket, TCB iş kolunun FY2026’da çeyreklik bazda en az %70 büyüyeceğini ve tek başına 100 milyon USD+ barajını aşacağını doğrulamıştır.

Fluxless TCB (FTC) ve Teknik Üstünlük

Gelişmiş veri merkezi işlemcilerinde kullanılan alt bileşenlerin kalınlıkları azaldıkça ve kalıp boyutları büyüdükçe (30 mm ve üzeri), geleneksel "flip-chip" yöntemleri termal eğrilme ve hizalama hatalarına neden olmaktadır. KLIC’in Fluxless TCB (FTC) teknolojisi bu problemi şu inovasyonlarla çözmektedir:

-- Mikron Altı Hassasiyet: Lehim tabanlı bağlantıları 10 μm hatveye (pitch), doğrudan bakır-bakır bağlantıları ise en az 5 μm hatveye kadar indirebilmektedir.

-- Formik Asit Buharı Oksit İndirgeme: Süreç esnasında sıvı akı (flux) kullanılmadığı için kalıplar arasında neredeyse "sıfır boşluk yüksekliği" elde edilir; bu da elektriksel direnci düşürür ve termal yönetimi optimize eder.

-- Co-Packaged Optics ve Silikon Fotonik: Yapay zeka kümelerindeki anahtarlama ve ağ hızlarını artırmak için optik transceiver bileşenlerinin doğrudan işlemci yanına TCB ile montajlanması sürecinde KLIC, küresel lider dökümhanelerden ve OSAT'lardan peş peşe siparişler almaktadır.

5. Kapasite Genişletme ve Gelecek Projeksiyonları

Advanced Solutions segmentinin ölçeklenebilmesi için üretim darboğazlarının aşılması gerekmektedir. Şirket yönetimi bu doğrultuda stratejik bir sermaye hamlesi yapmıştır:

1. CapEx Artırımı
CapEx Artırımı:FY2026 Dönemi.Şirket, yıllık sermaye harcaması (CapEx) bütçesini 12 milyon USD seviyesinden 22 milyon USD seviyesine neredeyse iki katına çıkarmıştır.

2. Üretim Hatlarının Modernizasyonu:
Q3/Q4 FY2026. Artırılan bütçe doğrudan Singapur ve ilgili üretim tesislerinde Fluxless TCB ve dikey tel montaj hatlarının otomasyon kalitesini yükseltmek için kullanılmaktadır.

3.Kapasite Hedefine Ulaşılması:
Erken FY2027. Yapılan bu yatırımlarla Advanced Solutions segmentinin yıllık üretim ve sevkiyat kapasitesi run-rate bazında 400 milyon USD satış potansiyeline ulaştırılacaktır.

Sipariş birikimini gösteren backlog yapısı da bu süreci desteklemektedir ve hızla artmakdadır. Siparişlerin iptal edilebilir olması bir risk olsa da Çin, Tayvan ve Kore'deki fabrika kullanım oranlarının %90'ın üzerinde seyretmesi, bu siparişlerin büyük ölçüde realizasyonla sonuçlanacağını göstermektedir.

6. Sektörel Karşılaştırma ve İleri Dönük Değerleme Modeli

Yarı iletken ekipman pazarında değerleme yapılırken ön uç (ASML, KLA, Lam Research, Applied Materials) ve arka uç (KLIC, Besi, ASMPT) ayrımı kritik rol oynar. Ön uç firmaları daha az döngüsel ve yüksek bariyerli oldukları için tarihsel olarak yüksek çarpanlara sahiptir.

KLIC ise tarihsel olarak olgun wire bonder pazarının dalgalanmalarına maruz kaldığı için düşük çarpanla işlem görmüştür. Fakat Advanced Solutions segmentinin kapasitesinin 400 milyon USD'ye ayarlanması ve TCB'nin yüksek karlılığı, çarpan genişlemesini tetiklemektedir.

Akran Grubu Karşılaştırma Analizi (FOTO - 1)

2027 Gelir ve Çarpan Projeksiyonu

-- FY2026 Tahmini Gelir: ~1.07 milyar USD (Mevcut çeyreklik gidişat ve Q3 kılavuzu tabanlı).

-- FY2027 Projeksiyon Geliri: Bellek toparlanması, ProMEM Suite adaptasyonu ve TCB'nin yıllık 100 milyon USD'lik barajı kırıp Advanced Solutions run-rate'ini yukarı taşımasıyla 1.25 milyar USD (%17-20 civarı bir yıllık büyüme).

-- Değerleme Yaklaşımı: Advanced Packaging payının artması sebebiyle şirkete hak ettiği tarihsel iskontonun üzerinde, 6x Forward P/S (İleri Dönük Satış) çarpanı uygulanması rasyoneldir. Bu çarpan altında şirketin adil kurumsal değeri 7.5 milyar USD olarak hesaplanmaktadır.

Şirketin kasasındaki 254.3 milyon USD net nakit ve sıfır borç yapısı, hisse başına değer hesaplamasında ekstra bir emniyet marjı sunmaktadır. Finansal modelleme kapsamında %8 iskonto oranı tatbik edildiğinde, hisse senedinin önümüzdeki 12 aylık periyotta 135 USD adil değer hedefine doğru re-rate edilmesi beklenmektedir. Bu da mevcut konsolide fiyata göre yaklaşık %20 yukarı potansiyel anlamına gelir.

7. Derinlemesine Risk ve Tehdit Analizi

Risk Matrisi ve Stratejik Tehditler: KLIC pozisyonlanmasında aşağı yönlü risklerin yakından izlenmesi gerekmektedir.

I. İkame Teknolojiler ve Hibrit Bağlama Tehdidi

En uç seviye AI hızlandırıcılarında (NVIDIA, AMD vb. yeni nesil mimarileri) TCB hatvelerinin dahi yetersiz kaldığı noktalarda Hybrid Bonding (Doğrudan Bakır-Bakır Die-to-Wafer entegrasyonu) devreye girmektedir. Bu alanda Hollandalı BE Semiconductor (Besi) tartışmasız pazar lideridir ve ana dökümhanelerin (TSMC vb.) onaylı tedarikçisidir.

Risk: Eğer Hybrid Bonding teknolojisi beklenenden daha hızlı ucuzlar ve orta segment çiplere de hızla adapte edilirse, KLIC'in TCB pazarı daralabilir. KLIC, 2024'ten bu yana kendi bünyesindeki Hibrit Bağlama AR-GE programını hızlandırdığını ve erken müşteri test aşamasında olduğunu (Mart 2026 güncellemelerinde) belirtse de Besi ile olan teknolojik ve zamansal makası kapatamama riski mevcuttur.

II. Müşteri Konsantrasyonu ve Kamçı Etkisi
KLIC’in gelir yapısı son derece dikeydir. En büyük 10 müşteri (büyük OSAT'lar olan ASE, Amkor ve dev IDM'ler) toplam grup gelirlerinin %55'ini oluşturmaktadır.

Risk: Son tüketici elektroniğindeki ani bir makroekonomik durgunluk, bu 10 dev firmanın CapEx bütçelerini anında dondurmasına yol açar. Arka uç üreticileri, tedarik zincirinin en sonunda yer aldığından "Kamçı Etkisi" nedeniyle sipariş iptallerini veya ertelemelerini en şiddetli hisseden grup olmaktadır.

III. Jeopolitik Sıkışma ve Doğu Asya Riski

KLIC her ne kadar Singapur merkezli ve ABD operasyonlu bir yapıya sahip olsa da en büyük yarı iletken montaj müşterileri ve dökümhaneleri Tayvan, Çin Halk Cumhuriyeti ve Güney Kore sınırları içerisindedir.

Risk: ABD-Çin ticaret savaşlarının gelişmiş paketleme ekipmanlarına (özellikle TCB ve alt bileşenlerine) yönelik getirebileceği ek ihracat yasakları veya Tayvan Boğazı'ndaki olası bir lojistik aksama, şirketin backlog teslimatlarını tamamen felç edebilir.

IV. CapEx ve Marj Baskısı

Yıllık CapEx'in 12 milyon USD'den 22 milyon USD'ye çıkarılması, kısa vadede nakit akışını ve serbest nakit akışı marjlarını (Q2 FY2026'da düzeltilmiş serbest nakit akışı 6.3 milyon USD seviyesindedir) baskılamaktadır. Yeni hatların verimlilik oranlarında yaşanabilecek herhangi bir operasyonel aksaklık, Q3 ve Q4 brüt marj tahminlerini aşağı yönlü revize ettirebilir.

8. Stratejik Sonuç

Kulicke & Soffa, döngüsel bir çöküşün ardından operasyonel karlılığa dönüş hikayesini başarıyla yazmıştır. Şirket, geleneksel iş kollarının sağladığı güçlü nakit akışını, geleceğin AI ve bellek standartları olan TCB, ProMEM ve dikey tel teknolojilerine başarıyla aktarmaktadır. Hisse senedinde son aylarda yaşanan güçlü momentum ve ralli, bu döngüsel dönüşün pazar tarafından satın alınmaya başladığını gösterse de FY2027 projeksiyonlarımız doğrultusunda şirketin çarpan büyümesinin henüz tamamlanmadığı değerlendirilmektedir. Bilançosunda borç bulunmayan, düzenli çeyreklik temettü ödeyen ve hisse geri alımlarıyla paydaş değerini destekleyen KLIC, uzun vadeli yarı iletken portföylerinde temel bir arka uç pozisyonu olarak öne çıkmaktadır.

text:
Sometimes I forget how rare investors like us are. 

I know many people in my country aren't even investing and have all their wealth on a savings account.

Then you have people who do invest, but only in indices. They add money occasionally and for the rest they never look back.

Then you have people who invest in stocks, but only the safe ones or dividend stocks, like $KO, $MCD, $OXY, etc.

And then you have us. Investors in AI, memory, photonics, space, you name it, with a very high amount of conviction. I've seen my portfolio go up or down over 10% on a single day several times this year and I barely flinch at this point.

We are a rare species.

$BURU Not financial advice. Not too often a penny stock catches my eye. the pivot from blue lasers to infusing those blue laser capabilities into counter UAS, drones, defense, software, photonics all under one roof to go along with their NATO ties for pennies on the dollar is quite fascinating. 

A lot of risks with the financial health that's true but upside could be worth it at these levels (currently $0.1481 per share ) Loaded 2100 shares yesterday at $0.1417. The more I research the more I'm thinking I should've bought more. Probably 10x my share count these next couple weeks.  

A lot of regret not catching $ONDS earlier (entered at $8). I personally like the defense sector as a whole at these low valuations, but $BURU seems like quite the sleeper.

Management is outlining a more than $300 million development plan to build an integrated defense and advanced technology platform, shifting the company away from being known primarily as a laser technology business. Think of it as a complete business transformation. Instead of relying mainly on industrial laser products, Nuburu wants to combine defense electronics, secure communications, AI, cybersecurity, drones, advanced manufacturing, and military technologies under one organization. If executed successfully, the company could evolve into a national defense technology platform rather than a niche hardware supplier. The timing is also important. Europe is rapidly increasing defense spending as NATO members commit to higher military budgets. Governments are looking to strengthen domestic defense supply chains instead of relying heavily on foreign suppliers. Tekne already has relationships in Italy’s defense sector, giving Nuburu an entry point into this growing market. Think about companies like $PLTR, $KTOS, $LHX, $LMT, $NOC, and $RTX. These businesses have generated strong long-term growth by providing software, autonomous systems, communications, sensors, and defense technologies. Nuburu appears to be positioning itself to participate in a similar ecosystem, although at a much earlier stage. The opportunity is significant because defense contracts are typically multi-year, recurring, and government-backed, providing greater revenue visibility than many commercial markets. However, investors should also recognize the risks. A turnaround of this size requires integrating acquisitions, securing new contracts, raising capital when needed, and successfully executing a completely new strategy. Until the company begins winning meaningful defense contracts and generating sustainable revenue, much of today’s valuation will depend on investor confidence in management’s execution. Bottom line: Investors are becoming interested because Nuburu is no longer being viewed solely as a laser company. If management successfully transforms it into an integrated European defense and advanced technology platform, the company’s long-term opportunity could be substantially larger than its legacy business. The next major catalysts will be contract wins, acquisition integration, government partnerships, and evidence that the $300 million development plan is translating into real revenue growth."}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2074769379749748791"}}, "rest_id": "2074783189860753420", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2020", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2076006116652102041", "source": "Twitter for Android", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1164", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$VECO — A semiconductor equipment company positioned at the intersection of AI, HBM, and advanced chips. Ideal entry zone $30-45.

• Manufactures critical semiconductor process equipment used for HBM/DRAM memory, advanced packaging, compound semiconductors, photonics, and AI-related chips.

• FY2025 revenue reached $664M, with semiconductor revenue representing 72% of total sales.

• AI-driven demand is accelerating opportunities in HBM memory, advanced packaging, and high-performance computing as chip complexity increases.

• Secured momentum in advanced semiconductor markets, including laser annealing systems for leading-edge logic and memory customers and continued adoption of new technologies.

• Trading at a much lower valuation than many AI semiconductor names, while providing exposure to the “picks and shovels” layer of the AI buildout.

AI needs more than GPUs.

It needs the equipment that builds the memory, packaging, and chips powering the next generation of compute.

text:
The launch of that fresh 2x short vehicle on $AAOI isn’t screaming “top” to me — it’s straight confirmation this name sits smack in the middle of the AI photonics arena. Shorts now have a clean leveraged tool to swing at it, and yeah, that could juice downside pressure during soft sessions with short interest hovering around 13-14%. But let’s get real about the setup.
$AAOI has a habit of ripping violent gaps on fresh order flow and hyperscaler momentum. If Q2 prints solid 800G ramp confirmation alongside any margin lift, this new short ETF flips from headwind to rocket fuel for squeezes. Near-term it amps volatility across the whole photonics crew — a dip in AAOI becomes the perfect excuse for traders to hammer related plays like $LITE, $COHR, $FN, $AXTI, $CRDO, and even the smaller ones such as $POET or $ALMU. As the highest-beta name in the group, AAOI now functions like the ultimate risk-on/risk-off toggle for the sector. Watch those flows closely — the battlefield dynamics favor the prepared.

text:
3. ¿Qué productos hace realmente?
Marvell se ha transformado en una compañía data infrastructure / AI-first. Sus productos principales hoy son:
•  Custom AI accelerators (XPUs) y silicio adjunto (XPU-attach, NICs, CXL).
•  Optical DSPs de alta velocidad (800G y 1.6T PAM4) → el motor de crecimiento actual.
•  Switches Ethernet de data center (Teralynx family, hasta 51.2 Tbps, baja latencia).
•  Interconnects de alta velocidad: Active Electrical Cables (AEC), PCIe retimers, CXL, silicon photonics y Co-Packaged Optics (CPO).
•  Storage controllers (HDD y SSD) — sigue siendo un negocio sólido pero ya no es el principal driver.
•  Otros (menor peso): DPUs, soluciones enterprise y carrier (5G).
El Data Center ya representa ~75-76% de los ingresos y crece mucho más rápido que el resto.

text:
2. Ventajas competitivas (el verdadero moat)
Marvell tiene varias capas de ventaja:
•  Liderazgo en óptica de alta velocidad: Es el referente mundial en PAM4 DSPs. Fue el primero en lanzar DSP de 1.6T en 5nm (Nova) y luego en 3nm (plataforma Ara). Sus productos Ara T, Ara X y Petra están ganando tracción masiva en 800G/1.6T para clusters de IA.
•  Custom Silicon / XPUs de primer nivel: Junto con Broadcom, es de las únicas dos compañías capaces de diseñar y llevar a producción en volumen ASICs/XPUs extremadamente complejos (cientos de miles de millones de transistores). Trabaja con AWS (Trainium), Microsoft (Maia), Meta (MTIA) y Google. Ofrece un servicio end-to-end (arquitectura + IP + diseño + packaging avanzado + soporte de fabricación).
•  Portfolio completo de data infrastructure: No solo hace una cosa. Tiene switches Ethernet de alto rendimiento (Teralynx hasta 51.2 Tbps), retimers PCIe 6 (Alaska P), CXL (Structera), Active Electrical Cables (AEC), Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) y silicon photonics. Es uno de los pocos que puede proveer casi todo el “nervio” de un data center de IA.
•  IP y tecnología propietaria fuerte: Excelente portafolio de SerDes de ultra alta velocidad, analog/mixed-signal de bajo consumo y tecnologías de packaging avanzado (chiplets, CPO, HBM custom).
•  Adquisición estratégica: Compró Celestial AI (óptica fotónica para scale-up interconnect), que potencia su posición en óptica de próxima generación.
•  Altas barreras de entrada: Los design wins de custom silicon son multi-generacionales y crean switching costs muy altos. Una vez que un hyperscaler diseña su XPU con Marvell, es muy difícil y caro cambiar.

text:
$MRVL (Marvell Technology) es uno de los grandes beneficiarios silenciosos (y cada vez más ruidosos) del boom de la infraestructura de IA. Aquí te detallo los puntos clave que pediste de forma clara y actualizada (datos a julio 2026).

1. - La inversión de NVIDIA ($2.000 millones)
El 31 de marzo de 2026, NVIDIA anunció una inversión de $2 mil millones en Marvell + una alianza estratégica profunda.
Marvell se integra al ecosistema NVIDIA a través de NVLink Fusion, lo que permite que los custom XPUs (aceleradores de IA) y soluciones de networking de Marvell funcionen de forma nativa y compatible con la infraestructura de NVIDIA (incluyendo Vera CPU, ConnectX NICs, BlueField DPUs, NVLink y Spectrum-X switches).
Además, colaborarán en silicon photonics y en llevar IA a redes de telecomunicaciones (AI-RAN para 5G/6G).
¿Por qué es tan importante?
•  Valida a Marvell como proveedor de custom silicon de primer nivel.
•  Da a los hyperscalers más flexibilidad: pueden usar GPUs NVIDIA + custom XPUs de Marvell en el mismo rack sin problemas de compatibilidad.
•  Es una “co-opetición” inteligente: Marvell ayuda a escalar el ecosistema NVIDIA mientras compite en custom ASICs y óptica.
El mercado reaccionó muy bien: MRVL subió fuerte ese día.

text:
Physicists Just Extracted Energy from a “Black Hole” — Without Any Rotation

In 1969, Roger Penrose proposed a mind-bending way to steal energy from a rotating black hole. Drop an object into the ergosphere (the zone just outside the event horizon), let it split, and one piece falls in while the other flies out with more energy than you started with. It sounded like science fiction.

More than 50 years later, researchers at the City University of New York have made a version of this effect real — in a lab, without any actual black hole or physical spinning.

The Breakthrough
Instead of building a rotating object, the team created a ring-shaped network of resonators whose properties change over time in a carefully controlled way (spatiotemporal modulation). This synthetic “motion” mimics rotation through the Floquet effect, allowing them to observe superradiance — waves coming out with more energy than they went in.

This is the first experimental confirmation of the Zel’dovich effect (an extension of Penrose’s idea) using purely electromagnetic waves in a non-rotating system.

Why It Matters
It bridges theoretical astrophysics with real-world photonics.
The experiment opens doors to new types of ultra-efficient amplifiers and wave generators.
It lets scientists study wave-matter interactions in rotating frames without needing massive, fast-spinning hardware.

Previous experiments used spinning disks or cylinders, but this new approach eliminates mechanical rotation entirely — a cleaner, more elegant demonstration.

While we’re still far from harvesting energy from real black holes, these tabletop analogs help us understand fundamental physics and could lead to revolutionary technologies in wave manipulation and energy harvesting.

Drop an object into the ergosphere (the zone just outside the event horizon), let it split, and one piece falls in while the other flies out with more energy than you started with. It sounded like science fiction. More than 50 years later, researchers at the City University of New York have made a version of this effect real — in a lab, without any actual black hole or physical spinning. The Breakthrough Instead of building a rotating object, the team created a ring-shaped network of resonators whose properties change over time in a carefully controlled way (spatiotemporal modulation). This synthetic “motion” mimics rotation through the Floquet effect, allowing them to observe superradiance — waves coming out with more energy than they went in. This is the first experimental confirmation of the Zel’dovich effect (an extension of Penrose’s idea) using purely electromagnetic waves in a non-rotating system. Why It Matters It bridges theoretical astrophysics with real-world photonics. The experiment opens doors to new types of ultra-efficient amplifiers and wave generators. It lets scientists study wave-matter interactions in rotating frames without needing massive, fast-spinning hardware. Previous experiments used spinning disks or cylinders, but this new approach eliminates mechanical rotation entirely — a cleaner, more elegant demonstration. While we’re still far from harvesting energy from real black holes, these tabletop analogs help us understand fundamental physics and could lead to revolutionary technologies in wave manipulation and energy harvesting."}}}, "rest_id": "2075646736484245804", "source": "Twitter Web Client", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "9440", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Rosenblatt releases bullish report on $AAOI and other optical names before earnings season.

The report cuts through the noise on why the optical sector has been weak the last few weeks. Two main fears stand out, both of which Rosenblatt sees as overstated. 

This looks like a huge buying opportunity.

1) China InP capacity

> The worry is that Chinese players are ramping hard and will flood the market with cheap lasers, hurting names like $LITE and $COHR.

> Chinese capacity could add 20-40% on top of the 2030 baseline, but supply still falls well short of demand.

> The Chinese are also still behind on the tech. Lower-end lasers are doing fine for 800G and below, but they don’t yet have enough production 200G EMLs for 1.6T+ transceivers or the high-power lasers needed for real CPO scale. 

> Western order books are full through 2027 and into 2028, with $NVDA securing a lot of the supply and $AMD now looking to the same names.

2) CPO timing

> The early smaller programs remain on track for 2H26 build and 2027 shipments. 

> The bigger scale-up volume looks more likely to hit in 2028, with NPO picking up a lot of the slack in 2027. 

> Other concerns like network flattening from larger switches were already modeled. Exploding XPU demand more than offsets any reduction in transceivers per switch. 

> VCSELs could play a role in some intermediate NPO designs, but high-power InP stays central for flagship CPO performance.

--------------------------------------------------------------

Short sellers: 

- One thing that stood out is what the short sellers are actually saying. Many have been using the China InP story to push the weakness, but most don’t appear to have strong conviction in it. 
- Multiple shorts told Rosenblatt they plan to close positions ahead of late July and early August earnings and then buy back into optical names in 2027 for the CPO ramp in 2028-2030. 

That’s not something you’d hear if they truly believed the oversupply thesis.

--------------------------------------------------------------

Overall, I feel this is bullish for optical names like $SIVE, $AAOI, $LITE, $COHR, $TSEM, $VIAV, etc. 

It also aligns with what I mentioned before that July end to August start should be a great time for optics as the earnings season plays out and some of this noise clears.

Long photonics!

Q: This seems like a quite complex field to understand.

A: The principle is very similar to urban planning. Frequently used data is placed close by, and occasionally used data is placed further away. Since speed is crucial when data moves, it's the principle of building a customized data transportation network—just like choosing a motorcycle or an express bus depending on the environment. A prime example of a global company specializing in building these communication networks is Broadcom.

Q: To the general public, tech companies might all look similar, but in reality, their unique roles and exclusive technological territories are firmly divided.

A: That's right. The key is to transfer data among HBM, HBF, and GPU as quickly as possible to reduce latency. Currently, data is transmitted as electrical signals through copper wires, but there is a physical limitation where the signal speed drops due to the copper's resistance. To overcome this, the method of transmitting data using 'light' (optical communication) instead of electricity has emerged, which is why silicon photonics-related companies are gaining attention recently. A prime example is the 'InfiniBand' technology acquired by NVIDIA, which handles optical communication between modules. Like the Strait of Hormuz, these monopoly technology companies, holding tight to the bottlenecks in the global chip supply chain, are positioned in their respective fields.

Q: When do you expect HBF to be commercialized?

A: Research is likely progressing with a target of 2027, so I expect the first product to be released around 2028. It is highly likely that one of NVIDIA, AMD, or Google will introduce the first commercialized product. The primary reason Big Tech companies are interested in HBF is to reduce their dependence on Korean companies. If they solely use HBM, they can't escape Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, but adopting HBF gives them alternatives to collaborate with American companies like SanDisk or Micron.

However, I believe that attempt will ultimately not hold much significance. I argue that to extract the absolute best performance, HBM and HBF must be configured together.

Q: If the environment requires both types of memory, doesn't that create an absolutely advantageous situation for Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, since they can produce both HBM and HBF?

A: Yes, it does. Because both companies already firmly possess world-class semiconductor stacking and packaging technologies through HBM production, they are naturally bound to hold the upper hand in the next-generation memory competition as well.

text:
The Trillion-Dollar Clean Energy Bottleneck: 5 Public Stocks Set to Fuel the Fusion Revolution

The man who lit up the world with the blue LED is about to power it with infinite clean energy.

Nobel Laureate Shuji Nakamura just officially unveiled his groundbreaking plan via Blue Laser Fusion to construct a massive 1-gigawatt laser fusion power plant by 2032.

The Disruptive Fusion Moat Checklist:

🔹 Lasers Over Magnets: Diverging from legacy magnetic confinement systems, Nakamura is utilizing specialized high-pulse inertial lasers amplified 100,000x via optical cavities to achieve self-sustaining energy net-gain.

🔹 Zero Radioactive Waste: The platform targets a non-radioactive hydrogen-boron (HB11) fuel cycle—meaning zero harmful neutrons, zero meltdown risks, and absolutely zero long-lived toxic waste.

🔹 The Ultimate Data Center Solution: As AI hyperscalers exhaust local grids, on-site, baseline, zero-emission laser fusion will become the holy grail infrastructure asset of the next decade.

While Blue Laser Fusion is currently a highly backed private venture (funded by SoftBank, Toyota's SPARX, and Itochu), the massive capital flows pouring into laser hardware, optics, and specialized power electronics are about to trigger a historic multi-billion dollar public supply chain supercycle.

The commercial race for infinite power has officially hit a structural inflection point. Are you positioned for the energy hardware boom, or still chasing speculative applications?

5 Public Stocks Set to Benefit from the Laser Fusion Boom:

1. Coherent Corp. (COHR) ⚡What they do / Potential: Manufactures the ultra-high-power industrial laser systems, optics, and precision crystal substrates required to generate and guide hyper-amplified beam pulses into the fusion fuel core.

2. IPG Photonics Corporation (IPGP) 🔬What they do / Potential: A global pioneer in high-power fiber lasers, uniquely positioned to supply the underlying high-efficiency, multi-kilowatt rapid-firing continuous laser modules necessary to scale up commercial power plants.

3. L3Harris Technologies, Inc. (LHX) 🛡️What they do / Potential: Builds complex photonic control systems, advanced target tracking tracking arrays, and optical cavities engineered to perfectly time microsecond laser impacts against fusion fuel pellets.

4. Materion Corporation (MTRN) 🧱What they do / Potential: Supplies advanced high-purity beryllium, specialized alloys, and thermal management metals critical to lining the internal walls of containment chambers to absorb and stabilize intense fusion energy output.

5. Perma-Fix Environmental Services, Inc. (PESI) ☢️What they do / Potential: While modern laser fusion is exceptionally clean, their specialized nuclear engineering and reactor waste maintenance frameworks will hold primary service value as standard infrastructure protocols roll out.

text:
AI 사이클 내년이후로 길게보면
엔비디아 TSMC 브로드컴
지표기업들
컴퓨팅파워와 속도 네트워킹.
ㅡㅡㅡㅡㅡ

[뉴스] TSMC의 PIC 생산 능력, 2028년까지 월 25,000장으로 급증 전망; 엔비디아와 브로드컴이 초기 COUPE 고객으로 거론됨

TSMC가 실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics) 분야로 사업을 확장하면서 시장의 관심이 쏠리고 있는 가운데, PIC(광집적회로, Photonic Integrated Circuits) 웨이퍼 생산 능력이 대폭 확대될 것으로 예상됩니다. 상업시보(Commercial Times)에 따르면, 기관 투자자들은 TSMC의 PIC 생산 능력이 월 약 500장 수준에서 2026년 2분기 10,000장으로 늘어나고, 4분기에는 15,000장으로 추가 확대되며, 2028년에는 월 최소 25,000장에 달할 것으로 추산하고 있습니다.

해당 보도에서 강조했듯, 초기 생산 램프업(생산량 확대) 기간 동안에는 PIC 생산 능력이 제한적이기 때문에 2026~2027년 TSMC의 COUPE 플랫폼 양산을 이용할 주요 고객은 엔비디아(NVIDIA), 브로드컴(Broadcom), AMD가 될 가능성이 높습니다. 2028년에 생산 능력이 더욱 확대되면, 미디어텍(MediaTek), 마벨(Marvell), 아야 랩스(Ayar Labs) 등 고객사의 CPO(공동 패키지 광학, Co-Packaged Optics) 프로젝트 역시 TSMC의 양산 플랫폼에 진입할 수 있을 것으로 보입니다.

PIC는 전기 및 광 신호를 변환, 라우팅, 결합하는 역할을 담당합니다. AI 서버 클러스터가 지속적으로 확장되고 스위치 대역폭이 25T 및 50T에서 100T 및 200T로 고도화됨에 따라 광 엔진에 대한 수요도 증가하고 있으며, 이에 따라 TSMC의 COUPE 플랫폼 진행 상황이 시장의 주요 관심사가 되고 있다고 보도는 덧붙였습니다.

이 보도에 따르면, 웨이퍼당 648개의 다이(die)가 생산된다고 가정할 때, TSMC의 월간 PIC 생산 능력이 500장에서 10,000장으로 늘어나면 연간 PIC 생산량은 약 400만 개에서 7,800만 개로 급증하게 됩니다. 생산 능력이 월 25,000장으로 더욱 확대될 경우, 연간 PIC 생산량은 약 1억 9,400만 개에 달할 수 있습니다.

이러한 전망을 바탕으로, 해당 보도는 TSMC의 PIC 생산 능력 확대가 갖는 세 가지 주요 시사점을 강조합니다.

1. CPO의 양산 진입: 
CPO 기술이 실험 및 소량 검증 단계를 넘어 점차 대량 생산 준비 단계로 나아가고 있음을 의미합니다.

2. 포괄적 AI 플랫폼 구축: 
실리콘 포토닉스와 첨단 패키징(Advanced Packaging)의 결합을 통해 COUPE, SoIC, CoWoS를 모두 결합한 더욱 포괄적인 AI 광전(optoelectronic) 플랫폼이 구축될 것으로 예상됩니다.

3. 관련 장비 수요 증가: 
PIC 생산량이 증가함에 따라 광섬유 어레이 유닛(FAU), 레이저, 광학 테스트 장비, 프로브 카드(Probe cards), 테스트 소켓 및 자동화 장비에 대한 수요를 견인할 것으로 예상됩니다.

상용화를 향해 나아가는 TSMC의 COUPE 플랫폼
경제일보(Economic Daily News)의 보도처럼, TSMC는 자사의 COUPE 플랫폼을 기반으로 한 세계 최초의 200Gbps 마이크로 링 변조기(MRM, Micro-Ring Modulator)가 2026년 하반기에 양산에 들어갈 예정이라고 발표했습니다. 
또한 TSMC는 이 소자가 공정 제어 조건 하에서 1E-08 미만의 비트 에러율(Bit Error Rate)을 달성했다고 밝혔습니다.

그러나 상업시보는 PIC 생산 능력 증가가 곧바로 CPO의 대규모 양산 램프업을 의미하는 것은 아니라고 지적합니다. 

다운스트림(후공정) 프로세스가 완성되기 위해서는 SoIC 통합, 광전 테스트, 광 엔진 패키징, FAU 결합, 시스템 수준의 검증 등 여러 단계를 아직 통과해야 하기 때문입니다.

월 25,000장으로 급증 전망; 엔비디아와 브로드컴이 초기 COUPE 고객으로 거론됨 TSMC가 실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics) 분야로 사업을 확장하면서 시장의 관심이 쏠리고 있는 가운데, PIC(광집적회로, Photonic Integrated Circuits) 웨이퍼 생산 능력이 대폭 확대될 것으로 예상됩니다. 상업시보(Commercial Times)에 따르면, 기관 투자자들은 TSMC의 PIC 생산 능력이 월 약 500장 수준에서 2026년 2분기 10,000장으로 늘어나고, 4분기에는 15,000장으로 추가 확대되며, 2028년에는 월 최소 25,000장에 달할 것으로 추산하고 있습니다. 해당 보도에서 강조했듯, 초기 생산 램프업(생산량 확대) 기간 동안에는 PIC 생산 능력이 제한적이기 때문에 2026~2027년 TSMC의 COUPE 플랫폼 양산을 이용할 주요 고객은 엔비디아(NVIDIA), 브로드컴(Broadcom), AMD가 될 가능성이 높습니다. 2028년에 생산 능력이 더욱 확대되면, 미디어텍(MediaTek), 마벨(Marvell), 아야 랩스(Ayar Labs) 등 고객사의 CPO(공동 패키지 광학, Co-Packaged Optics) 프로젝트 역시 TSMC의 양산 플랫폼에 진입할 수 있을 것으로 보입니다. PIC는 전기 및 광 신호를 변환, 라우팅, 결합하는 역할을 담당합니다. AI 서버 클러스터가 지속적으로 확장되고 스위치 대역폭이 25T 및 50T에서 100T 및 200T로 고도화됨에 따라 광 엔진에 대한 수요도 증가하고 있으며, 이에 따라 TSMC의 COUPE 플랫폼 진행 상황이 시장의 주요 관심사가 되고 있다고 보도는 덧붙였습니다. 이 보도에 따르면, 웨이퍼당 648개의 다이(die)가 생산된다고 가정할 때, TSMC의 월간 PIC 생산 능력이 500장에서 10,000장으로 늘어나면 연간 PIC 생산량은 약 400만 개에서 7,800만 개로 급증하게 됩니다. 생산 능력이 월 25,000장으로 더욱 확대될 경우, 연간 PIC 생산량은 약 1억 9,400만 개에 달할 수 있습니다. 이러한 전망을 바탕으로, 해당 보도는 TSMC의 PIC 생산 능력 확대가 갖는 세 가지 주요 시사점을 강조합니다. 1. CPO의 양산 진입: CPO 기술이 실험 및 소량 검증 단계를 넘어 점차 대량 생산 준비 단계로 나아가고 있음을 의미합니다. 2. 포괄적 AI 플랫폼 구축: 실리콘 포토닉스와 첨단 패키징(Advanced Packaging)의 결합을 통해 COUPE, SoIC, CoWoS를 모두 결합한 더욱 포괄적인 AI 광전(optoelectronic) 플랫폼이 구축될 것으로 예상됩니다. 3. 관련 장비 수요 증가: PIC 생산량이 증가함에 따라 광섬유 어레이 유닛(FAU), 레이저, 광학 테스트 장비, 프로브 카드(Probe cards), 테스트 소켓 및 자동화 장비에 대한 수요를 견인할 것으로 예상됩니다. 상용화를 향해 나아가는 TSMC의 COUPE 플랫폼 경제일보(Economic Daily News)의 보도처럼, TSMC는 자사의 COUPE 플랫폼을 기반으로 한 세계 최초의 200Gbps 마이크로 링 변조기(MRM, Micro-Ring Modulator)가 2026년 하반기에 양산에 들어갈 예정이라고 발표했습니다. 또한 TSMC는 이 소자가 공정 제어 조건 하에서 1E-08 미만의 비트 에러율(Bit Error Rate)을 달성했다고 밝혔습니다. 그러나 상업시보는 PIC 생산 능력 증가가 곧바로 CPO의 대규모 양산 램프업을 의미하는 것은 아니라고 지적합니다. 다운스트림(후공정) 프로세스가 완성되기 위해서는 SoIC 통합, 광전 테스트, 광 엔진 패키징, FAU 결합, 시스템 수준의 검증 등 여러 단계를 아직 통과해야 하기 때문입니다. https://t.co/w4FQ8IuzkB"}}}, "rest_id": "2074927905860427952", "source": "Twitter for Android", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "532", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
🇨🇦 Canada is quietly becoming one of the world’s biggest quantum computing hubs, and investors are starting to pay attention.

For years, Canada invested heavily in quantum research while much of the market focused on AI. Now that companies like $NVDA are openly embracing quantum computing, investors are beginning to realize that Canada may already have one of the strongest quantum ecosystems outside the U.S.

Since 2002, Canada has invested more than $2 billion into quantum technology, while the government recently committed another $334 million to accelerate development. That funding isn’t just supporting research—it’s helping build an entire industry spanning hardware, software, cybersecurity, networking, and future AI infrastructure.

One of the biggest names is $XNDU (Xanadu Quantum Technologies), which recently became Canada’s first publicly traded pure-play quantum computing company.

Unlike many competitors building superconducting quantum computers, Xanadu is developing photonic quantum computers, which use light (photons) instead of electrical circuits. Many researchers believe photonics could eventually offer better scalability, lower cooling requirements, and easier integration into future AI data centers.

Canada’s strength extends well beyond one company.

Companies like $BTQ, Quantum eMotion, Photonic, Nord Quantique, and Quantum Technologies are all building different pieces of the future quantum ecosystem, from post-quantum cybersecurity to fault-tolerant quantum hardware.

A major validation came when DARPA, the U.S. Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency, selected several Canadian quantum startups for its Quantum Benchmarking Initiative. DARPA is looking for technologies capable of building commercially useful quantum computers by 2033.

That recognition suggests Canadian companies are competing on a global stage—not just in academic research.

Why is $XNDU heavily shorted?

Despite the excitement, $XNDU currently has one of the highest short interests in the market, with over 70% of its free float sold short.

Short sellers believe the company’s valuation has moved far ahead of today’s business fundamentals. Revenue remains relatively small, profitability is still years away, and commercial quantum computing is still in its early stages.

Their argument is simple:

“The technology is promising—but investors may be paying tomorrow’s price for a company that is still building today’s products.”

However, the opposite argument is equally compelling.

If $XNDU continues hitting technical milestones, signs major commercial partnerships, or demonstrates meaningful progress toward scalable quantum computing, that extremely high short interest could fuel a powerful short squeeze, forcing bearish investors to buy back shares at much higher prices.

Wall Street remains constructive despite the volatility, with analysts maintaining a Moderate Buy rating and an average price target implying roughly 275% upside from recent trading levels.

The bigger picture

Think about AI 15 years ago.

Back then, most AI companies generated little revenue and many looked expensive. Investors who focused only on current earnings often missed the enormous opportunity that followed.

Quantum computing could be following a similar path.

Today it’s still experimental.

Over the next decade, it could transform drug discovery, financial modeling, materials science, cybersecurity, climate simulation, and AI itself.

There will likely be winners and losers along the way, but Canada has quietly positioned itself as one of the global leaders in this race.

For long-term investors, the real question isn’t whether quantum computing will matter.

It’s which companies will become the Nvidia, TSMC, or Microsoft of the quantum era. $XNDU is one of the companies trying to earn that position, making it one of the more closely watched—and most controversial—quantum stocks in the market today.

For years, Canada invested heavily in quantum research while much of the market focused on AI. Now that companies like $NVDA are openly embracing quantum computing, investors are beginning to realize that Canada may already have one of the strongest quantum ecosystems outside the U.S. Since 2002, Canada has invested more than $2 billion into quantum technology, while the government recently committed another $334 million to accelerate development. That funding isn’t just supporting research—it’s helping build an entire industry spanning hardware, software, cybersecurity, networking, and future AI infrastructure. One of the biggest names is $XNDU (Xanadu Quantum Technologies), which recently became Canada’s first publicly traded pure-play quantum computing company. Unlike many competitors building superconducting quantum computers, Xanadu is developing photonic quantum computers, which use light (photons) instead of electrical circuits. Many researchers believe photonics could eventually offer better scalability, lower cooling requirements, and easier integration into future AI data centers. Canada’s strength extends well beyond one company. Companies like $BTQ, Quantum eMotion, Photonic, Nord Quantique, and Quantum Technologies are all building different pieces of the future quantum ecosystem, from post-quantum cybersecurity to fault-tolerant quantum hardware. A major validation came when DARPA, the U.S. Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency, selected several Canadian quantum startups for its Quantum Benchmarking Initiative. DARPA is looking for technologies capable of building commercially useful quantum computers by 2033. That recognition suggests Canadian companies are competing on a global stage—not just in academic research. Why is $XNDU heavily shorted? Despite the excitement, $XNDU currently has one of the highest short interests in the market, with over 70% of its free float sold short. Short sellers believe the company’s valuation has moved far ahead of today’s business fundamentals. Revenue remains relatively small, profitability is still years away, and commercial quantum computing is still in its early stages. Their argument is simple: “The technology is promising—but investors may be paying tomorrow’s price for a company that is still building today’s products.” However, the opposite argument is equally compelling. If $XNDU continues hitting technical milestones, signs major commercial partnerships, or demonstrates meaningful progress toward scalable quantum computing, that extremely high short interest could fuel a powerful short squeeze, forcing bearish investors to buy back shares at much higher prices. Wall Street remains constructive despite the volatility, with analysts maintaining a Moderate Buy rating and an average price target implying roughly 275% upside from recent trading levels. The bigger picture Think about AI 15 years ago. Back then, most AI companies generated little revenue and many looked expensive. Investors who focused only on current earnings often missed the enormous opportunity that followed. Quantum computing could be following a similar path. Today it’s still experimental. Over the next decade, it could transform drug discovery, financial modeling, materials science, cybersecurity, climate simulation, and AI itself. There will likely be winners and losers along the way, but Canada has quietly positioned itself as one of the global leaders in this race. For long-term investors, the real question isn’t whether quantum computing will matter. It’s which companies will become the Nvidia, TSMC, or Microsoft of the quantum era. $XNDU is one of the companies trying to earn that position, making it one of the more closely watched—and most controversial—quantum stocks in the market today."}}}, "rest_id": "2074917486324314306", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "307", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Relax. Touch grass. Go find some extra cash.

$META isn’t waving the white flag on AI. They’re building a cloud business to monetize excess AI compute capacity. This is exactly what you do when your infrastructure investments are working and you have more power than you need internally right now. It’s a feature, not a bug. The market just spun it as oversupply panic.

On the $NVDA side, the comments around Kyber rack delays and some architecture tweaks are real noise. But Nvidia’s core roadmap stays intact. Rubin moves into broader availability in the second half of this year. Ultra and the following platform come in 2027-2028. No serious competitor catches up in high-performance AI accelerators before then. Every major model provider and hyperscaler is trying to build their own custom chips, but those won’t be available at meaningful scale until 2028. Custom silicon from the big clouds remains complementary for now.

Memory chips (especially HBM): AI data centers are still on track to swallow around 70 percent of global memory production in 2026. HBM is sold out through the year across $SKHY, Samsung, and $MU. This is a genuine supercycle.
AI data center buildout keeps accelerating. Hyperscalers are guiding for hundreds of billions in capex this year. The physical infrastructure demand for power, cooling, and space is only increasing.

Any increased focus on open models could ultimately prove very positive for the industry. Inference workloads from on-demand open models will drive demand for efficient, low-cost compute at specialized data centers. Providers like $NBIS and $IREN are well positioned here because they deliver cheap power and purpose-built infrastructure that these models will depend on. 

As noted on the All-In podcast, David Friedberg has highlighted that AI cloud services could host the majority of workloads (around 70 percent in his view), while local hosting persists and is not going away, especially with open models running on demand.

Photonics demand grows with the need for better efficiency. Co-packaged optics and silicon photonics solve power and bandwidth limits inside and across racks. Even with some near-term rack timeline shifts, the structural requirement for optical interconnects rises as clusters get larger.

Existing connected and powered capacity remains valuable. Volume ramps may shift later than some models assumed, but that just gives more time to accumulate quality names in memory, data center infrastructure, and photonics-related plays.

Hyperscaler earnings are coming up soon. Capex commentary will tell the real story. The music is still playing?

Touch grass. Go find some extra cash. $META isn’t waving the white flag on AI. They’re building a cloud business to monetize excess AI compute capacity. This is exactly what you do when your infrastructure investments are working and you have more power than you need internally right now. It’s a feature, not a bug. The market just spun it as oversupply panic. On the $NVDA side, the comments around Kyber rack delays and some architecture tweaks are real noise. But Nvidia’s core roadmap stays intact. Rubin moves into broader availability in the second half of this year. Ultra and the following platform come in 2027-2028. No serious competitor catches up in high-performance AI accelerators before then. Every major model provider and hyperscaler is trying to build their own custom chips, but those won’t be available at meaningful scale until 2028. Custom silicon from the big clouds remains complementary for now. Memory chips (especially HBM): AI data centers are still on track to swallow around 70 percent of global memory production in 2026. HBM is sold out through the year across $SKHY, Samsung, and $MU. This is a genuine supercycle. AI data center buildout keeps accelerating. Hyperscalers are guiding for hundreds of billions in capex this year. The physical infrastructure demand for power, cooling, and space is only increasing. Any increased focus on open models could ultimately prove very positive for the industry. Inference workloads from on-demand open models will drive demand for efficient, low-cost compute at specialized data centers. Providers like $NBIS and $IREN are well positioned here because they deliver cheap power and purpose-built infrastructure that these models will depend on. As noted on the All-In podcast, David Friedberg has highlighted that AI cloud services could host the majority of workloads (around 70 percent in his view), while local hosting persists and is not going away, especially with open models running on demand. Photonics demand grows with the need for better efficiency. Co-packaged optics and silicon photonics solve power and bandwidth limits inside and across racks. Even with some near-term rack timeline shifts, the structural requirement for optical interconnects rises as clusters get larger. Existing connected and powered capacity remains valuable. Volume ramps may shift later than some models assumed, but that just gives more time to accumulate quality names in memory, data center infrastructure, and photonics-related plays. Hyperscaler earnings are coming up soon. Capex commentary will tell the real story. Silver Mesh, Photonics and Datacenters
Last night's dream was about selecting the best candidate to work with another. The best candidate was what appeared to be silver in a mesh configuration. But there were other possibilities as displayed in different colors. Enter light. The silver was also specifically displayed in generations. 
So, what's the application (at least so far)? 
"Here's a visualization of a hybrid silver mesh + photonics network in a datacenter context (e.g., AI server infrastructure)." -Grok
"In short, silver mesh + photonics hybrids are a promising direction for next-generation datacenter components. They align with trends in silicon photonics, plasmonics, and hybrid electro-optic integration, potentially allowing even more efficient use of silver while delivering superior speed, power efficiency, and functionality. Research in transparent conductors, plasmonic modulators, and hybrid packaging continues to advance these concepts." 
To help understand the magnitude of silver usage in datacenters, I'll put a link in the🧵.

text:
Thoughts on the market and opportunities I see:
- Lot of FUD in the AI trade right now. This is healthy for the market. Many "bottlenecks" were hyped up (like photonics) and got overextended. Still remain ultra-bullish on memory for the next 6-12 months. Yes at some point, the supply/demand capacity imbalance will normalize but that will happen at the earliest in 2028.  Yes, there will be optimizations to how much memory is required both for model training and inference. But inference demand will grow massively especially as token price goes down which will make these companies not nearly as cyclical as they have been in the past.  Network effects here are underrated.
- The big opportunity in the AI trade is the orchestration layer. The layer that can execute user intent in a way that optimizes across cost, power, model capability, privacy, and latency.  The future wont require users to know model series or if they should use cloud provider A or neocloud B. I dont yet know who will win this but if they can focus, $AAPL is the best positioned because they are vertically integrated especially across devices (phone, tablet, airpods) and don't have a frontier lab they have to give preference to.
- Crypto is underpriced right now. The two big opportunities IMO are SoV (BTC/ZEC) and a way to create + run inference on models in a way that's not dependent on governments or OAI/Anthropic. As these labs get more nationalized, just like Bitcoin was an opt-out for people on government-issued money, accessing a distributed trained frontier model represents the same opt-out.  The technology is almost here to enable this, super exciting times (will write more about this in a future post)

text:
The silicon photonics supply chain diagram is one of the most important investment maps in technology right now (Save this).

Every AI data center being built depends on what's in that chart and the companies inside it are just starting to show what the revenue ramp looks like.

Here are the 10 stocks I am watching.

1. Broadcom (AVGO): Broadcom's AI revenue rocketed 106% to $8.4 billion in its most recent quarter, driven by custom accelerator chips for Google, Meta, and Anthropic, plus AI networking silicon. It is one of the few companies doing both the switch silicon and the photonics integration needed for co-packaged optics at hyperscale. The silicon photonics diagram shows Broadcom appearing across multiple layers, PIC design, laser sources, photodiode, and EIC design.

2. MACOM Technology (MTSI): MACOM is quietly positioning itself at the inflection point of the 1.6T and 3.2T optical transceiver transition. In March 2026 it launched 448G PAM4 modulator drivers among the first in the industry and joined Broadcom, Cisco, and Semtech in the 400G Optical MSA standards consortium. In June 2026 it introduced hot via chip scale technology, eliminating wire bonds in its AlGaAs packaging.

3. Marvell Technology (MRVL): Marvell is the DSP engine inside many of the optical modules in that chart. Its custom AI silicon, built for hyperscalers who want to own their accelerator architecture is the fastest growing part of its business. It sits at the EIC design layer and supplies the digital signal processing that makes high-speed optical links work reliably at scale. As the industry shifts from 400G to 800G and 1.6T, every step requires more sophisticated DSPs and Marvell captures more revenue per module shipped.

4. Keysight Technologies (KEYS): Every optical module that ships from every fab in that chart has to be tested before it reaches a data center. Keysight is the global leader in electronic and photonic test equipment. As optical speeds push to 800G and beyond, the testing instrumentation has to keep pace and it commands premium pricing.

5. FormFactor (FORM): FormFactor is the most underappreciated name in this entire chart. Q1 2026 revenue was $226 million, up 32% year over year, beating estimates with non-GAAP EPS of $0.56 versus $0.44 expected. It acquired Keystone Photonics in December 2025, becoming the leading wafer-level silicon photonics test platform for co-packaged optics production. Its partnership with Advantest created the world's fastest automated photonic alignment test system with nine-axis nano-precision. Over 100 of the world's leading silicon photonics manufacturers use FormFactor systems meaning every CPO chip that ships from any fab in that diagram gets tested on FormFactor equipment.

6. Teradyne (TER): Teradyne sits in the E/O Testing layer alongside Keysight and FormFactor. As silicon photonics chips get more complex, integrating lasers, modulators, photodetectors and electronic drivers on a single chip,  the test complexity explodes. Teradyne's automated test equipment platforms are expanding from traditional semiconductor testing into photonic integrated circuit validation. It also has a robotics division, which ties the silicon photonics thesis directly back to the humanoid supply chain story.

7. EXFO (EXFO): EXFO is a fiber optic and network testing specialist that has been building testing platforms specifically for coherent optical and silicon photonics applications. It sits directly in the E/O Testing box of the supply chain map. It's a smaller cap, which means the upside from the optical buildout is amplified relative to its size. It is one of the few companies that tests live network optical performance meaning it gets pulled in not just at the component manufacturing stage but every time a data center expands or upgrades.

8. Foxconn Industrial Internet (FXCOF): Foxconn appears twice in that supply chain diagram in both Photonics Assembly and Optical Interconnect and that is before you even consider its server business. Q1 2026 revenue hit $66.6 billion, up 29.7% year over year, with AI servers now representing more than 50% of total server revenue. Its silicon photonics CPO switches entered mass production in Q3 2026 with full year shipments forecast at 10,000 units. It is boosting capex 30% specifically for AI infrastructure. Almost no retail investors think of Foxconn as a silicon photonics play and that's the opportunity.

9. Sumitomo Electric (SMTOY): Sumitomo appears in two places on that supply chain diagram as an optical interconnect component supplier and in the laser/photodiode layer. The company has been a foundational supplier to the fiber optic industry for decades and is now scaling its silicon photonics packaging and optical connectivity capabilities for AI data center applications.

10. Synopsys (SNPS): Every silicon photonics chip has to be designed before it can be manufactured. Synopsys is the dominant EDA software company for photonic integrated circuit design and sits at the very top left of that supply chain map. The shift toward co-packaged optics means optical and electronic chip design have to be co-simulated, exactly the kind of complex, multi physics workflow Synopsys is built for. Its photonics design tools are already embedded across every major chip company in that diagram. The more complex the chip, the more customers pay for Synopsys tools.

Milk Road Pro is tracking every layer of the silicon photonics supply chain, from Broadcom and Marvell to the testing names most investors still don’t know.

Join Milk Road Pro for the full breakdown and for all our AI trades for just $1 using  the link below!

$SIVE is up ~1,700% in 2026, and following every tracked call on it returned +234% YTD on schwerelos.

Sivers makes the laser arrays for co-packaged optics, the interconnect AI data centers need to move data at 1.6 terabits. GlobalFoundries just signed them into its silicon-photonics platform, aimed at the $25B pluggable-optics market.

3 scored calls tracked, 1 closed in profit.

This innovation could impact #Semiconductor inspection. #Photonics

text:
Physical AI pick-and-shovel ⌁ $NOVT DD  🔖🛸

I spend a loooot of time evaluating the physical AI stack... with perhaps a slightly greater focus on humanoids.

Software models are expanding at an extraordinary pace, yet the immediate scaling constraint remains entirely hardware-bound.

Advanced whole-body control policies require instant, flawless translation into the physical world. 
It starts with perception, flows through planning and control, then reaches the physical world through motion, force feedback and actuation.

Every stage compounds error, so micro-second precision and adaptive force feedback determine whether an AI brain can function in reality or fail during contact-rich tasks.

The sim2real gap is a hardware execution problem... 

That's why I keep coming back to Novanta.

The company has built an ecosystem that occupies the exact center of this hardware constraint. 
They deliberately focus on manufacturing the core precision motion, sensing, and also photonics stacks that advanced robotics OEMs require.

🛸 ⌁ Motion stack
Precision motion from Celera Motion and Ingenia supplies the encoders (Aura series and IncOder CORE), frameless and slotless high-torque motors (Omni+ line), and certified servo drives (Denali and Everest Safe equipped with FSoE).

The result is (custom) actuators with sub-micron feedback and cogging-free smoothness paired with deterministic real-time control loops needed for stable locomotion and fluid multi-DOF manipulation. 

🛸 ⌁ Sensing stack
ATI Industrial Automation (one of their greatest acquisition) contributes multi-axis force/torque sensors from the Axia and Varo families together with automatic tool changers and material removal tools that turn position-controlled arms into systems capable of compliant grasping, adaptive manipulation, and importantly safe human interaction. 

These components matter for collision detection in unstructured environments and for force-modulated actions where pure kinematic control falls short.

🛸 ⌁ Photonics stack
The photonics capabilities, built around Cambridge Technology beam steering, Synrad CO2 sources, Laser Quantum solid-state and ultrafast lasers, plus ARGES scanning, support high-precision material processing. 

That matters both for manufacturing the next generation of robots and for the AI-optimized production cells those robots will eventually run inside factories and warehouses.

The laser sources, beam steering, scan heads, and related photonics technologies supplied by Novanta are also used in medical laser procedures, such as ophthalmology, dermatology/plastic surgery, soft tissue applications, and support for minimally invasive/robotic surgery systems.

🛸 ⌁ Healthcare stack (robotics surgery)
Continuing on the medical side, WOM insufflators, NDS visualization systems, but also integrated motion solutions underpin minimally invasive and robotic procedures. 

Riverpoint brings high-growth minimally invasive surgical consumables (like advanced surgical fibers, sutures, and implants for trauma and cardiovascular applications).

As AI moves into semi-autonomous subtasks in surgery to autonomous surgery, the same precision motion and force sensing that serve humanoids translate directly into safer, more consistent tissue interaction and better visualization pipelines, and vice-versa. 

🛸 ⌁ Final thoughts
What stands out is how the pieces fit together.

Acquisitions stitched a full motion and sensing stack, with end-effector tooling and supporting photonics into one offering. 

That integration reduces risk for OEMs who must hit safety and reliability thresholds for real-world deployments. 

Physical AI patform builders still do the system integration and higher-level control. Novanta supplies the subsystems that let those platforms actually perform at the edge of what physics allows.

$NOVT

In fact, it may actually give the company something extremely valuable:

Time.

* ramp

Whatever timeline doesn’t invalidate the Sivers thesis. In fact, it may actually give the company something extremely valuable: Time. Time to: * qualify products, * ramp manufacturing, * improve yields, * expand capacity, * secure customer approvals, * win additional design wins, * build a more resilient supply chain. No hyperscaler wants to wait until the year they need millions of lasers before suppliers start scaling production. Industrial capacity has to be built before demand peaks. We’ve already seen Sivers talking about expanding manufacturing, qualification progress, and preparing for high-volume production. Those milestones don’t disappear simply because volume deployment shifts by a few quarters. Between now and large-scale deployment there are still many potential catalysts: • customer qualifications • manufacturing milestones • capacity expansion • new partnerships • framework agreements • production updates • additional design wins Markets don’t wait for peak revenue. text:
Before a chip ships, it gets "burned in": baked under heat and voltage for hours so the ones destined to fail early die on the test bench, not in the field.

$AEHR (Aehr Test Systems) builds the machines that do this at the wafer level. It grew up testing the silicon carbide chips in EVs.

On July 9 its lead silicon photonics customer placed a follow-on production order for those systems, to burn in the optical transceivers that shuttle data between chips inside AI data centers.

Same testing step, now moving from EV power into the optical layer of the AI buildout. The stock has more than tripled in the past year. Fiscal Q4 numbers land July 14.

text:
Everyone remains fixated on the AI and hardware trade but $HOOD  and $SOFI  remain two of our favorite picks heading into the back half of 2026 (as well as two underrated performers of the year. 

We believe rate hike concerns are overblown at this time and that once the market adjusts to new fed norms and policy, banks will see a nice boost. 

Additionally, both Hood and Sofi are executing at a high level on their views of the digital banking future. Robinhood chain and Sofi USD have been smashing successes and are indicative of both companies intentions to be at the forefront of the new digital age. 

Both companies are still viewed as tied to one main revenue source (crypto for $HOOD and lending for $SOFI) but both have diversified rapidly and continue to push deeper and deeper into new sectors, while optimizing cross efficiencies between platforms and features. 

Both ecosystems are becoming stickier as SOFI expands its brokerage offerings, Robinhood continues to expand its banking offerings at a high level. 

These two fintechs may not be as exciting as neoclouds or photonics, but they remain future forward high growth names right at the cusp of a massive generational wealth transfer to millennials and gen z.

Additionally, both Hood and Sofi are executing at a high level on their views of the digital banking future. Robinhood chain and Sofi USD have been smashing successes and are indicative of both companies intentions to be at the forefront of the new digital age. Both companies are still viewed as tied to one main revenue source (crypto for $HOOD and lending for $SOFI) but both have diversified rapidly and continue to push deeper and deeper into new sectors, while optimizing cross efficiencies between platforms and features. 【半導体・AIインフラの地殻変動】 「AIの限界はソフトではなく物理（電力と信号）にある」
現在、AIクラスターが直面している「電力」と「光・信号」の圧倒的な物理的ボトルネック、そしてその裏で覇権を握る黒幕企業について、Techトレンドをまとめました。

半導体投資やインフラに関わる人は【ブックマーク】推奨です🧵👇

⚡ 電力のボトルネック：アナログの逆襲と物理の限界

アナログ半導体の時代到来：ムーアの法則が適用されないアナログ領域の価値が爆発中。TI、NXP、Infineon等の主要ベンダーは2026年に一斉値上げを断行。供給側が圧倒的な価格決定権を持つ市場へシフトしている。
12,500Aと800V直流の物理的限界：600kW〜1MW級の超高密度AIラックで従来の48V給電を続けると、電流は12,500Aに激増し熱損失で崩壊する。電流を750Aに抑え、銅材を45%削減できる「800V架構」への移行は物理的な必然。

VPD（垂直電力給電）：GPUコア（約0.7V）への降圧時の損失を防ぐため、電源変換器をGPUチップの「真下」に配置するVPD（電源界のCPO）が急速に台頭。
📡 信号・光学のボトルネック：銅の延命と光の現実

「光が銅を完全駆逐」は誤り：実際は距離に応じた住み分け。短距離（Scale-up領域）は銅線とアナログコンポーネントが粘り、長距離（ラック間接続）を光が担う。
Semtechのアナログ・マジック：200G/laneの超高速通信において、消費電力と遅延の元凶であるDSPを完全に排除し、アナログイコライザのみで信号を補正するSemtechの技術が激アツ。消費電力を10分の1に、遅延をns（ナノ秒）からps（ピコ秒）へと一気に縮小。

真のボトルネックは「光源」にあり：長期的な正解はCPOだが、今後2〜3年の現実的な勝者はNPOや1.6T可挿入型モジュール。本当のボトルネックはさらに下流の、InP（インジウムリン）基板、CWレーザー光源、Soitecが独占するPhotonics-SOIウェハ、そしてシリコンフォトニクス（SiPho）ファウンドリにある。
網 ネットワーク信頼性と「Link Flap」の恐怖

Link Flap現象がすべてを止める：光リンクが一瞬だけ瞬断する「Link Flap」が発生すると、AIクラスター全体の同期演算（all-reduce）が中断され、膨大なGPUが完全なアイドル状態（サボり）になる。
帯域よりも信頼性の時代へ：超巨大クラスターにおいて、単に帯域を広げるだけでなく、この瞬断を絶対に起こさない信頼性を保証できるソリューション（Credoの『Zero-Flap』など）が、AIビジネスの経済性を左右する最終ボトルネックになっている。

💡 まとめAIの進化は、最先端の1nm/2nmプロセスだけでなく、それを支える「電源（VPD/800V）」や「アナログ信号処理（Semtech等）」、「光源のサプライチェーン（Soitec等）」にいかに強みを持つかで勝負が決まるフェーズに入っています。
